title: fraxinus ornus subsp. argentea
intro: Fraxinus ornus subsp. argentea, also known as the silver ash, is a subspecies of the European ash tree, characterized by its distinctive silver-gray bark. The medicinal properties of silver ash lie in its bark, which contains a compound called fraxin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of silver ash include relief from pain, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Silver ash can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine and natural health practices.
